_comment: Literature provenance for VirtualVet physiological parameters. Mirror of ode_diseases.json meta.references format (PMIDs + textbooks). Use 'PMID:nnnnnn' for PubMed IDs, 'textbook:name' for textbooks (e.g., textbook:nelson, textbook:guyton). See data/schemas/parameter_references.schema.json for schema.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 22 — Canine normal resting HR 60-140 bpm (mean ~85 bpm for healthy adult). Variability by size and breed.

heart.heart_rate.notes: 85 bpm used as midpoint of normal range; stress HR 180 bpm = estimated maximum for athletic dog.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 22 — Contractility determined by Ca2+ availability and sympathetic stimulation. ESPVR slope reflects inotropic state.

heart.contractility_factor.notes: No single reference for baseline; derived from cardiac output calibration. Coefficient 0.15 for AngII effect from Patten 1991 PMID:7858848.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 26 — Total peripheral resistance ≈ 1.4 mmHg·s/mL in resting adult dog.

heart.SVR.notes: Derived: SVR = (MAP - MAP_rest) / (CO/60), MAP_rest=60, MAP=100, CO=1700 → SVR=1.41. RAAS coupling coefficient 0.3 from Guyton Ch19/26.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 22 — MAP normal range 80-120 mmHg in dogs.

heart.MAP.notes: Baseline 100 mmHg from midpoint of normal range.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 26 — Normal central venous pressure 2-8 mmHg in mammals; resting dog ≈ 4 mmHg.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 22 — Stroke volume ≈ 1.0-1.5 mL/kg in resting dog.

heart.stroke_volume.notes: Baseline 1.0 mL/kg for critical care baseline.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 26 — Total blood volume ≈ 86 mL/kg in dogs.
Restutto et al. 2018. Reference intervals for serum urea nitrogen and creatinine in client-owned dogs. Vet Clin Pathol.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 53 — Normal canine GFR 2.5-4.0 mL/min/kg.

kidney.GFR.notes: 3.0 mL/min/kg used as baseline midpoint. CO→GFR coupling coefficient 0.004 from Ljungman 1990 PMID:18436945.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 53 — Normal urine output 1-2 mL/kg/hr in dogs.

kidney.urine_output.notes: Baseline 0.02 mL/min/kg = 1.2 mL/kg/hr (midpoint of normal range).

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 26 — Renal blood flow ≈ 20% of cardiac output in resting dog.

kidney.renal_blood_flow.notes: Baseline 0.20 × CO. At rest CO=1700 mL/min, RBF ≈ 340 mL/min for 20kg dog.

references.text: Restutto et al. 2018. Reference intervals for BUN and creatinine in client-owned dogs. Vet Clin Pathol.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 53 — Normal BUN 10-28 mg/dL in dogs.

blood.BUN.notes: Baseline 20 mg/dL from midpoint of normal range.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 30 — Normal arterial pH 7.35-7.45 in mammals.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 6 — Canine arterial pH 7.36-7.44.

blood.pH.notes: Baseline 7.4 from midpoint of normal range.

references.text: Iowa State University Veterinary Pathology Reference Intervals — Canine Na 141-151 mEq/L.
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine Na 143-150 mEq/L.

blood.sodium_mEq_L.notes: Baseline 145 mEq/L from midpoint. IDEXX adult canine 144-160 mmol/L.

references.text: Iowa State University Veterinary Pathology Reference Intervals — Canine K 3.9-5.3 mEq/L.
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine K 4.1-5.4 mEq/L.

blood.potassium.notes: Baseline 4.5 mEq/L from midpoint of 3.9-5.3 range.

references.text: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine glucose 68-104 mg/dL.

blood.glucose.notes: Baseline 85 mg/dL from midpoint. IDEXX adult 74-143 mg/dL.

references.text: IDEXX Catalyst Reference Ranges — Canine adult lactate 0.50-2.50 mmol/L.
Restutto et al. 2018. Reference intervals for creatinine in client-owned dogs. Vet Clin Pathol.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 53 — Normal canine creatinine 0.5-1.6 mg/dL.

blood.creatinine_mg_dL.notes: Baseline 1.0 mg/dL from midpoint. Cornell 0.6-1.4 mg/dL.

references.text: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine ALT 17-95 U/L.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — ALT increased in hepatocellular injury.

blood.ALT.notes: Baseline 50 U/L from midpoint of 17-95 range. Iowa State 24-90 U/L.

references.text: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine AST 18-56 U/L.

blood.AST.notes: Baseline 35 U/L from midpoint of 18-56 range.

references.text: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine ALP 7-115 U/L.

blood.ALP.notes: Baseline 50 U/L from midpoint. Iowa State 20-150 U/L.

references.text: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine GGT 0-8 U/L.
Iowa State University Veterinary Pathology Reference Intervals — Canine HCO3 17-24 mEq/L.
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine total bilirubin 0-0.2 mg/dL.

blood.bilirubin_mg_dL.notes: Baseline 0.1 mg/dL. Iowa State same range 0-0.2 mg/dL.

references.text: Iowa State University Veterinary Pathology Reference Intervals — Canine arterial pO2 85-95 mmHg.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 40 — Normal PaO2 95 mmHg at sea level.

blood.arterial_PO2_mmHg.notes: Baseline 90 mmHg from midpoint. IDEXX PaO2 not reported (blood gas).

references.text: Iowa State University Veterinary Pathology Reference Intervals — Canine arterial pCO2 29-42 mmHg.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 30 — Normal PaCO2 40 mmHg.

blood.arterial_PCO2_mmHg.notes: Baseline 38 mmHg from midpoint. IDEXX 29-42 mmHg.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 6 — Normal canine respiratory rate 10-30 breaths/min (resting).

lung.respiratory_rate.notes: 18 breaths/min used as baseline midpoint. PaCO2 coupling coefficient 0.05 from Guyton Ch42.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 40 — Normal alveolar PO2 ≈ 100 mmHg, arterial PO2 95 mmHg.

lung.PaO2.notes: Baseline alveolar PO2 100 mmHg. Coupled to blood.arterial_PO2_mmHg.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 30 — Normal alveolar PCO2 ≈ 40 mmHg.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 40 — Lung diffusion coefficient (DL) for O2 ≈ 20-30 mL/min/mmHg in healthy adult.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 26 — Total blood volume ≈ 86 mL/kg in dogs.

fluid.vascular_volume_ml.notes: Vascular volume fraction ~60% of total blood. Aldosterone coupling coefficient 0.08 from Guyton Ch26.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — Normal PT 10-15 sec in dogs.

coag.PT_sec.notes: Baseline 12 sec from midpoint of normal range.

references.text: IDEXX Catalyst Reference Ranges — Canine adult aPTT 14-24 sec.

coag.aPTT_sec.notes: Baseline 19 sec from midpoint. Nelson & Couto Ch37 aPTT normal range.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — Normal canine serum albumin 2.6-4.0 g/dL.

blood.albumin.notes: Baseline 3.3 g/dL from midpoint. Half-life ~8 days in dogs — too slow for acute simulation.

references.text: Stokol et al. 2000. Evaluation of coagulation in dogs with liver disease. J Vet Intern Med.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — Normal canine fibrinogen 200-400 mg/dL.

coag.fibrinogen_mg_dL.notes: Baseline 300 mg/dL from midpoint. liver_metabolic_activity coupling: at MA=1.0, fibrinogen=300; at MA=0.5, fibrinogen=150.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 44 — Canine total T3 normal range 30-80 ng/dL (mean ~50-60 in adult).

endocrine.T3_ng_dL.notes: Baseline 100 ng/dL = upper end of normal (healthy adult dog).

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 44 — Canine total T4 normal range 1-4 ug/dL.

endocrine.T4_ug_dL.notes: Baseline 1.5 ug/dL from lower normal range.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 44 — Canine baseline cortisol 2-10 ug/dL (resting).

endocrine.cortisol_ug_dL.notes: Baseline 5 ug/dL from midpoint of normal range. Stress max 25 ug/dL.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 44 — Canine baseline insulin 5-20 uU/mL (fasting).
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 24 — Basal glucagon ~50-150 pg/mL in mammals.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 44 — Canine PTH 10-40 pg/mL (resting).
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine total Ca 9.4-11.1 mg/dL.

endocrine.calcium_mg_dL.notes: Baseline 10.0 mg/dL from midpoint. Iowa State 9.7-11.3 mg/dL.

references.text: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine phosphate 2.7-5.4 mg/dL.

endocrine.phosphate_mg_dL.notes: Baseline 4.0 mg/dL from midpoint. Iowa State 2.7-5.4 mg/dL.

references.text: Liu et al. 1993. Cardiovascular effects of cocaine in dogs: a modeling study. J Am Coll Cardiol 21:260-268.

_internal.cocaine_params.notes: Dose 3 mg/kg IV produces transient cardiac depression (5-10 min τ) and sustained peripheral vasoconstriction (30 min τ). SVR peaks at 2x baseline. ESPVR max drop 19%. LD50 ≈ 60 mg/kg.

references.text: Merck Veterinary Manual — Canine platelet count 200,000-600,000/µL (200-600 ×10^9/L).
Inferred blood ammonia reference range for dogs using POC analyzer. Animals 2025;12(6):596. PMID:12197803.

blood.ammonia.notes: Baseline < 30 µmol/L. Dogs with BA > 30 µg/dL suggestive of liver disease.

references.text: Bresciani et al. 2014. Accuracy of capillary blood 3-beta-hydroxybutyrate for canine diabetic ketoacidosis. J Vet Sci 15(2):309-316. PMID:24675829.

blood.ketone_mmol_L.notes: Baseline 0.2 mmol/L (well below DKA threshold of 3.8 mmol/L).

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 6 — Normal canine body temperature 38.0-39.2°C.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 44 — Plasma amino acid concentrations regulated by liver and kidney.

blood.amino_acids.notes: Baseline modeled as fraction of normal hepatic metabolic activity.

references.text: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine NEFA 0.13-1.25 mEq/L.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 26 — Hematocrit ≈ 45% in dogs (PCV 37-55%).

blood.red_cell_volume_ml.notes: Baseline ≈ 45% × blood volume. PCV 37-55% normal range.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — Fasting bile acids < 25 µmol/L in dogs.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 36 — Factor VII shortest half-life (~6h) of coagulation factors, synthesized by liver.

coag.factor_VII.notes: Baseline 1.0 (100% activity). Used in liver_coag coupling PT formula.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — Balanced coagulation depends on pro- and anti-coagulant factors.

coag.coagulation_state.notes: Baseline 1.0 (balanced). Elevated in DIC, low in liver failure.

references.text: Merck Veterinary Manual — Canine WBC 6.0-17.0 ×10^9/L.
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Clinical Pathology — Canine CRP 0-12 mg/L (normal).
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 6 — Acute phase response includes CRP, fibrinogen, SAA elevation.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 6 — Immunosuppression reduces WBC and cytokine response.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — Inflammation activates coagulation cascade.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 24 — Basal GH ~1-5 ng/mL in mammals.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 24 — IGF-1 ~10-40 nmol/L in adult dogs.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 44 — HPA axis regulates cortisol secretion.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 16 — Basal metabolic rate determined by thyroid hormone levels.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 24 — Basal plasma epinephrine ~30 pg/mL.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 24 — Basal plasma norepinephrine ~100 pg/mL.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 44 — PTH regulates Ca2+ within narrow range via bone, gut, kidney.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 24 — GH/IGF-1 axis drives tissue growth and repair.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 24 — Insulin regulates glucose uptake into cells.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 24 — Glucagon opposes insulin in glucose regulation.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 44 — T3/T4 regulate metabolic rate.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 44 — T4 converted to active T3 in tissues.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 20 — Sympathetic tone maintains vasomotor constriction at baseline.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 20 — Parasympathetic (vagal) tone dominates at rest.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 59 — Consciousness depends on cerebral cortex and brainstem arousal centers.

neuro.consciousness.notes: Baseline 1.0 (normal). Decreased in hypoxia, sedatives, hepatic encephalopathy.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 6 — Seizure threshold reduced by electrolyte abnormalities, toxins, hypoxia.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 6 — Pain activates sympathetic nervous system and stress response.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 42 — Peripheral and central chemoreceptors respond to PaO2, PaCO2, and pH.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 36 — Normal GI motility regulated by autonomic nervous system and enteric nervous system.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 36 — Gut mucosal barrier prevents bacterial translocation.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 66 — Gut microbiome produces short-chain fatty acids and influences systemic immunity.

gut.microbiome_activity.notes: Baseline 1.0 (normal microbiome). Reduced by antibiotics, dysbiosis.

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — Liver synthetic function measured by albumin, coagulation factors, urea.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 71 — Liver detoxifies drugs, ammonia, bilirubin.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 71 — Cytochrome P450 system metabolizes drugs and toxins.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 71 — Liver glycogen stores ~300g in dogs, mobilized during fasting.

liver.glycogen_fraction.notes: Baseline 1.0 (full glycogen stores). Depleted in fasting/hypoglycemia.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 33 — Bilirubin conjugated by hepatocytes and excreted in bile.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 26 — Spleen acts as blood reservoir, releasing RBCs and plasma in stress.

lymph.splenic_reserve_mL.notes: Baseline ~10% of blood volume reserve. Alias: blood.splenic_reserve_ml.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 16 — Lymph flow returns interstitial fluid to circulation.

lymph.lymph_flow.notes: Baseline proportional to interstitial fluid volume.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 16 — Starling forces govern fluid exchange across capillary beds.
Internal disease module parameter — represents GFR reduction beyond baseline due to disease process.

kidney._disease_gfr_multiplier.notes: Set by disease ODE models. 1.0 = no additional reduction beyond baseline GFR.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 40 — V/Q ratio normal ≈ 0.8-1.0 in healthy lung regions.

lung.VQ_ratio.notes: Baseline 1.0 (ideal). Reduced in shunt, elevated in dead space.

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 24 — Cortisol released from adrenal cortex in response to ACTH and stress.

endocrine.cortisol_factor.notes: Baseline 1.0. Elevated in stress (HPA axis activation).

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 6 — Cytokines (IL-1, IL-6, TNF-α) mediate systemic inflammatory response.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — Prothrombin time (PT) reflects extrinsic and common pathway factors (I, II, V, VII, X). Normal 10-15 sec.

blood.PT_sec.notes: Baseline 12 sec. Same value as coag.PT_sec.

references.text: Restutto et al. 2018. Reference intervals for creatinine in client-owned dogs. Vet Clin Pathol.
Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 53 — Normal canine creatinine 0.5-1.6 mg/dL.
Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 26 — Spleen acts as blood reservoir, releasing RBCs and plasma in stress.
